# GitHub Topics / Keywords

Add these topics to your GitHub repository settings for better discoverability:

## Primary Topics (Top 3-4)

1. **nats** - Core technology
2. **jetstream** - NATS JetStream focus
3. **monitoring** - Primary use case
4. **observability** - Core feature

## Secondary Topics

5. **messaging** - Message queue/monitoring
6. **dashboard** - Web UI
7. **golang** - Backend language
8. **react** - Frontend framework
9. **kubernetes** - K8s deployment
10. **helm** - Helm charts
11. **docker** - Container deployment
12. **stream-processing** - Stream monitoring
13. **message-broker** - Message queue monitoring
14. **devops-tools** - Operations tooling
15. **admin-panel** - Administrative interface
16. **real-time** - Real-time updates (SSE)
17. **time-series** - Metrics/timeseries
18. **websocket** - Real-time communication
19. **open-source** - Open source project

## Niche Topics

20. **nats-io** - NATS ecosystem
21. **streaming** - Data streaming
22. **event-streaming** - Event-driven architecture
23. **microservices** - Microservice monitoring
24. **admin** - Administration tool
25. **metrics** - Metrics collection
26. **analytics** - Data analytics
27. **visualization** - Data visualization
28. **ui** - User interface
29. **typescript** - Frontend TypeScript
30. **go** - Go programming

## How to Add Topics

1. Go to your repository on GitHub
2. Click Settings → Topics
3. Search and add the topics listed above

Maximum topics: 20 (choose the most relevant)

## Recommended Selection (Top 20)

```
nats, jetstream, monitoring, observability, messaging, dashboard,
golang, react, kubernetes, helm, docker, stream-processing,
message-broker, devops-tools, admin-panel, real-time, time-series,
nats-io, microservices, metrics
```
